what_it_does: The presentation stylesheet for the Tonal Zoning System deliverable page (Tonal Zoning System.html). It composes the canonical zone tokens into the page's layout: masthead, section scaffold, the zone-registry card grid, the component-role gallery, a shared component vocabulary (cvx-btn / cvx-chip / cvx-vi), and the applied Overview dashboard mock (nav / main / Vi rail) with a spatial grid/path layer. It explicitly invents no colour, only arranges zone roles.
observations: body background layers a radial --zone-base-strong gradient over --zone-base-surface; a fixed body::before draws a faint architectural grid (64px) masked top-weighted and scaled by --spatial-opacity
Masthead styles (.tz-mast/-title/-lede/-key) with display font, italic bronze emphasis, and a gold-left-border 'key' callout on --vi-surface
.tz-registry is an auto-fill minmax(232px) grid of .tz-zone cards showing a wash chip + strong + edge + ink dot per zone; hover lifts the card

Shared component vocabulary: .cvx-btn (--primary gold, --secondary on panel surface, --reject on reject zone), .cvx-chip (ready/pending/source/reject zone-coloured pills with a currentColor dot), .cvx-vi (gold gem pill on --vi-strong)

Applied Overview: .ov is a fixed 1240px 3-col grid (232 / 1fr / 320) with transform-origin top-left for JS scaling; .ov-spatial / .ov-flow path layers gated by --spatial-opacity; .tz-ghost off-frame neighbour frames

Zoned blocks (.ov-block.content/.source/.package) take their zone surface+edge; stats use gold numerals, the flagged inbox stat takes the warning zone; nav active item gets a gold left-border + content-strong background; the Vi rail rides --vi-surface with gold gems
Stat/tile/feed cards use #fff backgrounds with faint borders and shadow tokens
standouts: Strict adherence to 'nothing here invents colour' — every surface/edge/ink comes from var(--zone-*-*) or --vi-* tokens
Gold concentrated exactly where the system reserves it: primary buttons, stat numerals, nav active marker, the entire Vi rail and its gems
Spatial-layer opacity (--spatial-opacity) threaded through the body grid, ghost frames, and both SVG path layers so the whole connective story toggles as one knob
conventions: var(--token) referencing throughout for color, radius (--r-*), shadow (--shadow-*), type (--font-*), tracking; consumes the canonical token homes
Zone-role composition pattern (surface + strong + edge + ink per zone) applied uniformly across cards, chips, buttons, blocks
BEM-ish cvx- component prefix and tz- page prefix separating shared vocabulary from page chrome
concerns: Repeated literal background:#fff on cards/stats/tiles/feed rows/pills rather than a token (e.g. a paper/surface var) — a small recurring hardcode in an otherwise token-pure file
The .ov fixed width 1240px and grid columns are duplicated knowledge with the host HTML's DESIGN_W=1240 scaling constant — two places to keep aligned
A handful of raw rgba/px values (e.g. the 0 40px 80px -40px rgba(31,26,18,.28) shadow on .tz-applied-frame) sit alongside shadow tokens
notes: The deliverable's presentation CSS: lays out the tonal-zoning specimen (masthead, zone-registry grid, component-role gallery, shared cvx- component vocabulary, and the applied Overview dashboard with its spatial layer) purely by composing canonical zone tokens.
questions: Should the repeated #fff card backgrounds be a token (paper/surface) to stay fully single-sourced, or is white intentional for specimen cards?
Is the 1240px design width meant to live in one place shared with the host page's scaler rather than being restated in both?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — the header states it consumes canonical tokens and only composes zone roles for the deliverable presentation, and the file does exactly that for the specimen and the applied dashboard.
apparent_themes: Reserved-meaning gold concentrated in Vi / decision / active marks
Felt-not-seen spatial texture toggled by one opacity knob
Zone-role uniformity across every component and block
intention_signals: Trying to render the tonal-zoning language convincingly to the eye — a polished editorial specimen plus a believable re-skinned product dashboard — while proving discipline by sourcing every colour from the zone tokens and concentrating gold only where the system permits.
novelty: The applied Overview re-skin that demonstrates zone roles in a real dashboard layout (zoned blocks, gold-only Vi rail, off-frame ghost neighbours, toggleable spatial path layer) entirely from composed tokens.
contribution: Contributes the presentation/layout composition for the tonal-zoning specimen — the masthead, zone-registry grid, the shared cvx- component vocabulary (buttons/chips/Vi pills), and the fully zoned applied Overview dashboard — showing how the zone tokens assemble into real screens.
summary_for_embedding: A ConceptV CSS stylesheet for the Tonal Zoning System deliverable page that composes canonical zone tokens into a masthead, zone-registry grid, a shared cvx- component vocabulary, and an applied Overview dashboard mock with a toggleable spatial path layer.
intention_for_embedding: To present the tonal-zoning language to the eye as a polished specimen and a re-skinned product dashboard, composing every colour from the zone tokens and reserving gold for Vi and active decisions.
